features.title: Full ECHO Release Stack
features.playerPromise: Run the public ECHO release stack as one coordinated workspace instead of isolated test mods.
features.loreContext: Ashfall is the first terrestrial campaign, while Core, Terminal, Orbital, Agriculture, Stationfall, Nexus, Industrial, and Blackbox extend the same post-Gridfall continuity.
features.implementationSummary: The real Gradle modules are seeded, grouped, scanned, built, and jar-managed as one full-stack project.
features.nextAction: Keep this aligned with settings.gradle and the jar manifest whenever modules change.

evidence.label: Command Center seeds 13 expected ECHO jars
evidence.detail: The echo project module list drives full-stack release and jar actions.

features.title: Crash-To-Nexus Core Loop
features.playerPromise: Start at the crash pod, stabilize survival, recover route intel, defeat guardians, and reach the Nexus choice before the addon chain opens.
features.loreContext: ECHO-7 wakes after Gridfall under a damaged field protocol and follows the Nexus signal back through ruined Earth.
features.implementationSummary: The plan and release smoke notes define the complete loop; Command Center can verify resources and release actions but does not yet prove every gameplay beat from inside Minecraft.
features.nextAction: Use deep scan, GameTests, and manual smoke notes to close the remaining in-game proof gap.

evidence.label: Full stack smoke notes cover terminal, guardians, Nexus, and addon entry checks
evidence.detail: Smoke coverage is documented but still needs run evidence per release.

features.title: P1-P9 Progression Ladder
features.playerPromise: Move from crash setup through survival infrastructure, POIs, guardians, Nexus, orbit, and the public release chapter chain.
features.loreContext: The ladder turns post-collapse survival into ECHO's escalating recovery, quarantine, and memory arc.
features.implementationSummary: The progression ladder is documented and mapped to modules; readiness remains tied to scans, jars, and release gates rather than a dedicated progression simulator.
features.nextAction: Attach release run evidence to each ladder segment after full-stack verification.

evidence.label: P1-P9 progression table
evidence.detail: The plan assigns each major phase to survival, Nexus, and addon milestones.

features.title: Hazard Zone Survival
features.playerPromise: Treat toxic swamps, industrial ruins, radiation zones, cryogenic ruins, and Nexus scars as readable environmental pressure systems.
features.loreContext: Gridfall changed biomes into faction-owned, machine-scarred danger regions.
features.implementationSummary: The hazard model is documented and represented by module scans and smoke checks; not every hazard beat is independently summarized by Command Center yet.
features.nextAction: Record deep scan and GameTest evidence for each hazard type.

evidence.label: Quick scanner checks resources, runtime logs, and jar expectations
evidence.detail: Command Center can catch missing assets and jar blockers for hazard content.

features.title: Toxic Air And Filtration
features.playerPromise: Make toxic air a zone pressure that players counter with filters, safe zones, and infrastructure.
features.loreContext: Industrial collapse and Sporebound contamination leave air itself as part of the survival puzzle.
features.implementationSummary: Toxic-air expectations are in the mod plan and Industrial Nexus scrubbers provide cross-chapter support, but Command Center still treats this as feature evidence rather than a completed release gate.
features.nextAction: Link latest validator/GameTest runs that prove toxic-air helper paths and scrubber modes.

evidence.label: Industrial Scrubber lists air, radiation, blight, station, and cooling modes
evidence.detail: Scrubber behavior is part of the Industrial Nexus release promise.

features.title: Radiation Pressure
features.playerPromise: Let radiation build, punish unsafe exploration, and reward cleansing gear, routes, and machines.
features.loreContext: Radwarden tracks the radiation scars left by Gridfall control nodes and Nexus exposure.
features.implementationSummary: Radiation appears in Ashfall, Orbital suit survival, Industrial scrubbers, and Nexus lore, but the app does not yet aggregate per-system runtime proof.
features.nextAction: Add release-run evidence to distinguish implemented mechanics from plan coverage.

evidence.label: Radiation is referenced by survival, Orbital suit telemetry, and Industrial scrubber checks
evidence.detail: The feature spans several modules.

features.title: Hydration And Clean Water
features.playerPromise: Make water cleanup and hydration part of the early survival economy.
features.loreContext: The ruined world makes basic human survival infrastructure an ECHO recovery objective.
features.implementationSummary: The plan calls out hydration and water purification; Industrial Nexus extends purifier tooling, while full in-game release proof remains external to the feature catalog.
features.nextAction: Attach deep scan or GameTest evidence for water purifier and hydration paths.

evidence.label: Water Purifier appears in Industrial automation lists
evidence.detail: Machine-side support is documented.

features.title: Mutation Instability
features.playerPromise: Make Nexus scars and long-term exposure push the player toward unstable but readable mutation consequences.
features.loreContext: The Nexus does not only corrupt terrain; it rewrites living systems and ECHO's final choices.
features.implementationSummary: Mutation behavior is present in the mod plan as a survival pillar, but Command Center has no concrete implementation evidence to mark it complete.
features.nextAction: Keep this as planned until code/resources or release checks prove the mechanic.

evidence.label: Mutation instability is described as a planned survival system
evidence.detail: No stronger implementation evidence was found during catalog seeding.

features.title: Machines And Automation
features.playerPromise: Turn salvage into power, water, filters, parts, and late-game materials through visible machines and recipe guidance.
features.loreContext: Recovered machinery is the practical language of rebuilding after Gridfall.
features.implementationSummary: Ashfall machine notes, JEI coverage, Terminal Recipe Index, and Industrial Nexus processing are documented; release evidence is still split across modules.
features.nextAction: Use full-stack GameTests and recipe scans to prove the whole automation surface.

evidence.label: JEI and Terminal Recipe Index coverage is part of smoke notes
evidence.detail: Machine recipes are expected in both JEI and Terminal.

features.title: World, POIs, And Route Exploration
features.playerPromise: Give every route and biome a reason to scan, recover caches, find records, and return with better tools.
features.loreContext: ECHO's world is a field archive: every ruined structure is part of a broken command history.
features.implementationSummary: The plan covers POIs, route maps, faction sites, and smoke checks. Command Center can find resource/reference issues but not yet certify worldgen quality.
features.nextAction: Run deep validators and manual world smoke passes for POI coverage.

evidence.label: Ashfall structure README files are present
evidence.detail: Structure docs support the exploration plan.

features.title: Factions And Intel
features.playerPromise: Expose Radwarden, Crashbreak, Sporebound, and chapter factions through atlas entries, route records, and reward state.
features.loreContext: The factions interpret Gridfall differently and turn survival choices into political telemetry.
features.implementationSummary: Faction canon and Terminal surfaces are documented; Orbital and Logistics include faction-facing evidence, but the catalog conservatively treats the full-stack feature as partial.
features.nextAction: Attach latest terminal and chapter GameTest evidence for faction records.

evidence.label: Terminal Faction Atlas and route records are listed as shared surfaces
evidence.detail: The player-facing intel surface is part of the stack plan.

features.title: ECHO-7 Terminal Surface
features.playerPromise: Use one shared terminal for command, progress, intel, system diagnostics, recipes, rewards, routes, and addon chapter tabs.
features.loreContext: ECHO-7 is both guide and witness, translating damaged command protocol into player-facing decisions.
features.implementationSummary: The Terminal module documents concrete APIs, navigation groups, mission providers, recipe providers, themes, route records, archives, vitals, and addon integration.
features.nextAction: Keep terminal page coverage tied to readiness and scan findings.

evidence.label: Terminal APIs and resources are housed in the echoterminal module
evidence.detail: README names mission, action, archive, reward, vitals, route, recipe, and theme surfaces.

features.title: Drone System
features.playerPromise: Use drones as recoverable utility actors for scouting, delivery, and field support.
features.loreContext: Autonomous systems are old ECHO infrastructure learning to function in a broken world.
features.implementationSummary: The full-stack plan has a drone section, while concrete evidence is currently stronger in Logistics courier drones than in the broad ECHO feature.
features.nextAction: Promote this to partial when the full-stack drone loop has project-level smoke evidence.

evidence.label: Logistics Network contains courier drone implementation evidence
evidence.detail: This proves module-level courier behavior, not the entire full-stack drone plan.

features.title: Research And Perk Guidance
features.playerPromise: Make research unlocks, schematic hints, locked outputs, and late-game knowledge visible before players have every item.
features.loreContext: Research is how ECHO converts field ruins into actionable survival knowledge.
features.implementationSummary: Terminal Recipe Index and Nexus Protocol research are documented; the broad feature remains partial until run evidence is attached.
features.nextAction: Use scan findings and release runs to distinguish recipe visibility from research unlock completion.

evidence.label: Recipe Index locked schematic hints are documented
evidence.detail: The research surface is tied to recipe visibility and Nexus progression.

features.title: Nexus Endgame Choices
features.playerPromise: Reach the Nexus Core and commit Restore, Control, Destroy, or Merge with visible consequences across later chapters.
features.loreContext: The Nexus choice is the first irreversible answer to whether ECHO repairs, controls, destroys, or joins the broken system.
features.implementationSummary: The plan defines guardian gates, Nexus states, and cross-chapter interpretations, but the app should continue to require release evidence before calling the whole endgame complete.
features.nextAction: Run Nexus Protocol smoke checks and full-stack release verification.

evidence.label: Nexus Protocol smoke checks enumerate gates, machines, boss, and choices
evidence.detail: The planned acceptance path is explicit.

features.title: Release Addon Chapter Chain
features.playerPromise: Continue after Ashfall through Orbital, Agriculture, Stationfall, Nexus Protocol, Industrial Nexus, and Blackbox Protocol.
features.loreContext: The addon chain widens ECHO from crash survival into orbit, station horror, infrastructure, memory, and final authorship.
features.implementationSummary: The overview documents the chain and individual chapter promises; module-level feature rows show which chapters have stronger local evidence.
features.nextAction: Open each module project and use its feature rows plus release actions for chapter-specific completion.

evidence.label: Full stack smoke notes list chapter checks
evidence.detail: Stationfall, Nexus, Industrial, and Blackbox all have named smoke paths.

features.title: Shared ECHO Services
features.playerPromise: Let addons share state through Core services instead of reaching into each other's saves.
features.loreContext: ECHO Core is the hidden protocol layer that lets every chapter speak the same recovery language.
features.implementationSummary: Core is seeded as a real module and is described as the owner for shared contracts, state, and cross-addon infrastructure.
features.nextAction: Keep new addons behind public Core APIs rather than private chapter state.

evidence.label: echocore is an expected full-stack jar
evidence.detail: Command Center jar management derives a Core artifact from project metadata.

features.title: Ashfall Survival Campaign
features.playerPromise: Deliver the terrestrial crash survival campaign that starts the ECHO saga and opens the Nexus branch.
features.loreContext: Ashfall is ECHO-7's first field recovery route on ruined Earth.
features.implementationSummary: Ashfall owns the root workspace and large portions of the plan; Command Center tracks its scoped build, validation, resources, and jar expectations.
features.nextAction: Use Ashfall scoped scans and full-stack GameTests to prove route, guardian, and Nexus coverage.

evidence.label: Ashfall resources live in the root workspace
evidence.detail: The scanner uses root resources for the Ashfall module.

features.title: Shared Command Surface
features.playerPromise: Provide terminal block, menu, mission routing, navigation sections, archives, rewards, route records, diagnostics, faction atlas, and vitals.
features.loreContext: ECHO-7 turns fragmented command authority into a practical player interface.
features.implementationSummary: The README lists the public APIs and player surfaces that the module ships.
features.nextAction: Keep deferred badge/notification APIs visible as future polish rather than current implementation.

evidence.label: Terminal API and theme systems are documented
evidence.detail: The README names mission providers, action routing, navigation profiles, recipe providers, and theme tokens.

features.title: Reusable SignalOS Terminal Framework
features.playerPromise: Give modpacks a reusable terminal framework for missions, archives, rewards, diagnostics, and chapter UIs.
features.loreContext: SignalOS is tooling, but it supports the same command-console grammar ECHO uses.
features.implementationSummary: SignalOS documents shipped APIs, JSON/datapack registration, KubeJS bridge support, and known current limitations.
features.nextAction: Do not treat deferred theme rendering as shipped UI behavior.

evidence.label: SignalOS currently ships chapter, mission, archive, reward, diagnostics, and KubeJS surfaces
evidence.detail: The README differentiates current support from limitations.

features.title: SignalOS Integration Sample
features.playerPromise: Show concrete Java, datapack, and KubeJS integration paths for SignalOS.
features.loreContext: This example is not ECHO canon; it is implementation guidance for command-surface authors.
features.implementationSummary: The example README lists three integration paths and sample registration snippets.
features.nextAction: Keep examples compiling when SignalOS public APIs change.

evidence.label: README shows Java API, datapack JSON, and KubeJS-friendly usage
evidence.detail: The example is intentionally focused on integration coverage.

features.title: Orbital Route Survival Arc
features.playerPromise: Follow ECHO-7 from Earth recovery to orbit, outer route worlds, ECHO-0, faction contracts, and the final survey network seal.
features.loreContext: After the Nexus choice, orbit proves Earth was never only a containment field.
features.implementationSummary: Orbital's release status marks the route survival arc complete as a standalone NeoForge space-survival chapter, with implemented systems and release checks listed.
features.nextAction: Treat planetary RPG, bespoke animated encounters, and settlement/vendor expansion as future scope.

evidence.label: PLAN says the standalone space-survival arc is complete
evidence.detail: Implemented systems include progression, launch, routes, surveys, threats, factions, CI, docs, and GameTests.

features.title: Nexus Protocol Chain
features.playerPromise: Open after Stationfall's blackbox handoff, expose Nexus research, machines, dimension/worldgen, monoliths, bosses, and final commitments.
features.loreContext: Nexus Protocol turns the first Ashfall choice into deeper Core instructions and world-state consequences.
features.implementationSummary: The overview and public smoke pass describe the chapter; Command Center should still rely on scoped scans and release actions for current proof.
features.nextAction: Run the Nexus scoped quick scan and release smoke pass before calling it complete.

evidence.label: Nexus systems and smoke checks are documented
evidence.detail: The plan names machines, state, worldgen, boss, and commit actions.

features.title: Field Reclamation Farming Loop
features.playerPromise: Recover seeds, stabilize crops, run hydroponics, score greenhouse safety, and restore ruined-world farming routes.
features.loreContext: Agriculture Reclamation makes survival recovery physical: food, soil, genes, and ecology pressure.
features.implementationSummary: The README documents standalone NeoForge addon behavior, Terminal/Core integration, route milestones, crop loops, and 0.1.1 utility outputs.
features.nextAction: Keep scans attached to mature-only crop drops, hydroponics, and greenhouse checks.

evidence.label: README names FIELD > Reclamation metrics and six route milestones
evidence.detail: The chapter has concrete recovery mechanics and terminal integration.

features.title: Station ECHO Horror Route
features.playerPromise: Board Station ECHO, recover logs, manage oxygen/pressure/panic telemetry, override the AI, defeat Station Mother, and hand off the blackbox.
features.loreContext: Stationfall reveals the station did not merely die; it kept recording the fall.
features.implementationSummary: The overview and local lore code show the chapter shape, but the feature catalog keeps it partial until scoped scan and GameTest evidence are current.
features.nextAction: Run Stationfall quick scan and confirm station boarding, nine sections, logs, AI override, and blackbox recovery.

evidence.label: Station lore class exists

features.title: Blackbox Memory Finale
features.playerPromise: Decode fragments, survive archive dungeons, confront hostile recordings, assemble Core access, and choose final directives.
features.loreContext: Blackbox Protocol asks who gets to author the final memory after ECHO and the Nexus have both failed trust.
features.implementationSummary: The overview describes the finale, structures, bosses, machines, and directives; current status remains partial without fresh module scan evidence.
features.nextAction: Run scoped scan and release checks for memory stability, monolith access, bosses, and Truth Engine directives.

features.title: Industrial Automation Recovery
features.playerPromise: Build Thermal Flux infrastructure, ducts, recipe-driven machines, scrubber safe zones, heat handling, industrial POIs, and Furnace Warden progression.
features.loreContext: Industrial Nexus rebuilds the world through machinery while heat and corruption keep that recovery dangerous.
features.implementationSummary: The README documents standalone play, optional sibling hooks, machine systems, terminal recipe integration, overheating, scrubbers, boss progression, and an RC note that validation/build/GameTests pass in the checkout.
features.nextAction: Keep optional compat matrix checks visible when sibling chapters are absent or present.

evidence.label: README RC note says validation, compile, build, and shared GameTests pass
evidence.detail: This is strong local evidence for implemented status.

features.title: Storage, Loadout, And Courier Logistics
features.playerPromise: Use labeled storage networks, expedition loadouts, courier drones, depot offers, relay rewards, and SYSTEM > Logistics terminal support.
features.loreContext: Logistics turns recovery into base operations: supplies, routes, drones, and field readiness.
features.implementationSummary: The smoke test and module resources show concrete blocks, items, courier drone code, terminal support, and GameTest coverage.

evidence.label: Courier drone and Logistics GameTests exist

features.title: Convoy Route Layer
features.playerPromise: Add ruined-Earth travel through field vehicles, fuel, cargo, roadside route contracts, checkpoint gating, and FIELD > Convoy Routes terminal support.
features.loreContext: Convoy Protocol makes the wasteland traversable after the player can maintain a vehicle and protect supplies.
features.implementationSummary: The README documents the feature promise and a smoke path covering vehicle workbench, fuel, cargo, repair, beacon activation, contracts, rewards, and Terminal route actions.
features.nextAction: Run scoped scan and GameTests before including a release build.

features.title: Hidden Resonance Scanning
features.playerPromise: Scan hidden resonance in blocks, entities, items, biomes, dimensions, weather, and landmarks.
features.loreContext: ARCANA starts from the idea that the world is full of Veil patterns the player cannot see until studied.
features.implementationSummary: The README and creative checklist define the scanning loop; status is partial until current resource validation and GameTests are attached.

features.title: Research Theory And Node Graph
features.playerPromise: Build research theory through graph nodes, active research details, hidden nodes, and claimable discoveries.
features.loreContext: ARCANA treats knowledge as something assembled, not unlocked by a simple checklist.
features.implementationSummary: The README documents research JSON with optional journal layout metadata and automatic placement fallback; the checklist covers tabs, graph pages, nodes, and claims.
features.nextAction: Run resource validator to confirm research layout and datapack fallback behavior.

features.title: Field Journal UI
features.playerPromise: Open a textured two-page research book with Resonance, Discoveries, Research, Rituals, Machines, Fractures, Constructs, and Paths tabs.
features.loreContext: The journal is the player's hand-written interface to Veil study.
features.implementationSummary: The README describes the runtime GUI texture location and the asset audit confirms the Field Journal and machine screens are code-rendered with the intended visual language.
features.nextAction: Keep browser-like manual creative checks for missing textures and tooltip coverage.

evidence.label: Asset audit confirms Field Journal and machine screens are code-rendered
evidence.detail: The audit names dark panels, copper borders, violet headers, teal status text, pressure bars, and fracture warnings.

features.title: Ambient Veil Pressure
features.playerPromise: Let local Veil pressure become a readable world-state that rituals, fractures, machines, and monitors respond to.
features.loreContext: The Veil is not a spell list; it is environmental pressure that must be shaped or contained.
features.implementationSummary: The README names ambient Veil pressure as core design, and the checklist includes Veil Monitor and Fracture Detector checks, but current implementation proof is not summarized here yet.
features.nextAction: Attach validator/GameTest evidence for Veil Monitor, Fracture Detector, and pressure mutations.

evidence.label: Checklist includes Veil Monitor and Fracture Detector status text
evidence.detail: Pressure feedback has a manual acceptance target.

features.title: Ritual And Convergence Work
features.playerPromise: Run Ritual Basin recipes, Focus Pedestals, Convergence Matrix recipes, and Stabilizer Pillar-supported convergence work.
features.loreContext: ARCANA magic is procedural and risky, with ritual geometry and stabilization as part of the cost.
features.implementationSummary: The checklist covers ritual and convergence actions, and the asset audit reports data entries for rituals and convergence.
features.nextAction: Run resource validation and one creative test pass for Ritual Basin and Convergence Matrix flows.

features.title: Fracture Risk And Containment
features.playerPromise: Expose fracture samples, seals, detectors, transforms, entities, and pressure reduction as the cost of pushing Veil work too far.
features.loreContext: Fracture is the shadow of research: every discovery can tear the world open.
features.implementationSummary: The checklist covers Fracture Seal use, Fracture Detector status text, fracture entities, and Fracture Heart; the asset audit reports fracture transforms and no intentionally missing assets.
features.nextAction: Run validator and runtime log checks to prove fracture resources and transforms remain clean.

evidence.label: Audit says no registered item, block, or entity intentionally lacks model, texture, lang, or loot coverage

features.title: Sigil Constructs
features.playerPromise: Assign constructs to carry, guard, scan, maintain rituals, deliver, repair, and harvest through Sigil Plates.
features.loreContext: Constructs are ARCANA's practical proof that theory can be embodied.
features.implementationSummary: The asset audit reports practical construct behaviors with held-item persistence, while the creative checklist names assignment and order cycling.
features.nextAction: Keep animation/proportion polish tracked as future art polish, not blocker status.

evidence.label: Sigil Constructs support practical carry, deliver, guard, scan, and ritual maintenance behaviors

features.title: Assets And Resource Coverage
features.playerPromise: Ship authored Minecraft-scale textures, models, lang, loot, GUI, entity, particle, worldgen, and research resources without intentional missing coverage.
features.loreContext: ARCANA's identity depends on readable ritual tools, Veil color language, fracture warnings, and journal surfaces.
features.implementationSummary: The asset audit says runtime assets cover every registered item, block, and entity and no registered item/block/entity is intentionally missing model, texture, lang key, or loot coverage.

features.title: Worldgen Landmarks
features.playerPromise: Discover Deep Veil Gates, observatories, vaults, sealed archives, ritual circles, and fractured groves through structure discovery.

features.title: Late-Game Veil Choices

features.title: ARCANA Verification Pipeline

features.title: Shared Archive Browser
features.playerPromise: Browse item, recipe, usage, tutorial, lore, research, and archive records through a shared Index surface.
features.loreContext: The Index is ECHO knowledge made searchable: salvage, recipes, machines, tutorials, and research in one field archive.
features.implementationSummary: Source code registers the Index service, builtin provider, recipe provider, and Terminal integration with a shared archive summary.
features.nextAction: Run Index scoped scan and GameTests before promoting the jar.

evidence.label: Index registers service, builtin provider, recipe provider, and Terminal integration
evidence.detail: The source summary is Shared item, recipe, usage, and archive browser.

features.title: Armory Loadout Readiness
features.playerPromise: Equip modular weapons, armor, augments, energy cores, mission loadouts, faction gear, and Terminal-managed combat readiness.
features.loreContext: Armory turns ECHO recovery into survivable combat preparation for toxic, radiation, fracture, convoy, and orbital threats.
features.implementationSummary: Mod metadata describes the module promise, resources define gear/modules/loadouts/synergies, and Terminal integration registers an Armory tab with readiness metrics and actions.
features.nextAction: Run Armory scoped scan and GameTests, then promote after Minecraft releases the current mods folder locks.

evidence.label: Gear, modules, logistics loadouts, and synergies are defined as data resources
evidence.detail: Resources include armory gear, modules, loadouts, logistics presets, faction unlocks, and synergies.

evidence.label: Terminal Armory tab reports modules, protection, loadouts, and combat actions
evidence.detail: The integration exposes scan, equip, install, stance, recharge, preview, and logistics actions.

features.title: Scanner HUD
features.playerPromise: Inspect blocks, entities, fluids, machines, progression, and addon context through a compact modern ECHO HUD.
features.loreContext: Lens makes field intelligence immediate: operators read the world directly instead of opening a separate archive for every target.
features.implementationSummary: Gradle metadata describes the scanner HUD, Index resources document the player-facing overview, GameTests cover provider ordering and block/entity sections, and Core integration registers the inspection service.
features.nextAction: Run Lens scoped scan and GameTests, then include echolens-1.0.0.jar in the next clean modpack promotion.

evidence.label: Index entry says Lens scans blocks, entities, and fluids
evidence.detail: The overview also documents safe public inventory behavior.

evidence.label: GameTests cover provider ordering and block/entity section generation
evidence.detail: Tests assert provider order, duplicate rejection, safe failure handling, block sections, entity sections, and Core registration.

features.title: Facility Multiblock Runtime
features.playerPromise: Form, validate, diagnose, persist, and operate facility-scale multiblocks with runtime snapshots, robotic task queues, workcells, integrity, blueprints, and optional ECHO UI/intel integrations.
features.loreContext: MultiblockCore turns ECHO facilities from static ruins into rebuildable infrastructure with controllers, diagnostics, robotics, maps, scans, and mission-facing state.
features.implementationSummary: The README documents v3 integration services, JSON definitions, controller runtime behavior, persisted task queues, diagnostics, public APIs, map markers, scan providers, and debug commands.
features.nextAction: Run MultiblockCore scoped scan, GameTests, and promote echomultiblockcore-1.0.0.jar in the next clean modpack update.

evidence.label: README documents integration facade, validation diagnostics, runtime snapshots, and task queues
evidence.detail: The module has a concrete public API and runtime behavior for facility multiblocks.

evidence.label: Smoke notes cover module validation and runtime acceptance
evidence.detail: The smoke test document gives release-facing verification steps.

features.title: Runtime Performance Manager
features.playerPromise: Keep the ECHO modpack responsive with runtime modes, honest TPS/MSPT/FPS reports, smart tick budgets, particle budgets, multiblock validation queues, network accounting, and opt-in throttles for expensive addon work.
features.loreContext: RuntimeGuard is the operational layer that keeps ECHO field systems from overwhelming fragile recovered hardware while still explaining what it can and cannot observe.
features.implementationSummary: The README documents TPS/MSPT monitoring, client FPS monitoring, Potato/Balanced/Cinematic/Server/Debug/Emergency modes, smart tick recommendations, particle budgeting, multiblock validation queueing, network budget tracking, block entity/entity throttles, reports, commands, public APIs, and optional MultiblockCore/HoloMap/Lens integrations.
features.nextAction: Run RuntimeGuard scoped scan, GameTests, and promote echoruntimeguard-1.0.0.jar in the next clean modpack update.

evidence.label: README documents runtime modes, smart ticks, particle budgets, validation queues, and network budgets
evidence.detail: The module has concrete player/admin commands and opt-in APIs for first-party addon performance management.

evidence.label: Smoke notes cover commands, reports, permissions, and addon integrations
evidence.detail: The smoke test backs release validation for commands, RuntimeGuard reports, server-safe behavior, and MultiblockCore/HoloMap/Lens integrations.

evidence.label: RuntimeGuard API and runtime services exist in source
evidence.detail: Source includes RuntimeGuardServices, RuntimeGuardProfiler, SmartTickService, ParticleBudgetService, MultiblockValidationScheduler, NetworkBudgetService, and command/report classes.

features.title: Structural Block Library
features.playerPromise: Build ECHO bases, ruined cities, crash zones, convoy depots, orbital interiors, Terminal rooms, Blackbox vaults, Nexus structures, reclamation domes, and MultiblockCore casings with first-party block families and conversion tools.
features.loreContext: Blockworks gives every ECHO chapter a consistent material language, from ash-choked starter ruins to orbital hulls, Blackbox vaults, Nexus crystal, and reclamation glass.
features.implementationSummary: The README documents ten block families, structural variants, detail blocks, Blockworks Table conversion, Pattern Cutter cycling, tags, recipes, palette kits, rare showcase worldgen, procedural scatter, Index integration, and development tooling.
features.nextAction: Run Blockworks scoped scan, GameTests, asset/resource validation, and promote echoblockworks-1.0.0.jar in the next clean modpack update.

evidence.label: README lists block families, detail blocks, conversion systems, palette kits, and worldgen
evidence.detail: The module has concrete player-facing building content and generated resources.

evidence.label: Smoke notes cover Blockworks release validation
evidence.detail: The smoke test document backs scoped QA and release acceptance.
